commit.message:
Refactor syslog structured-data parsing for Check Point dialect (#5851)

## Summary
- support common Check Point structured-data variants in RFC 5424 syslog
records
- normalize missing SD-ID payloads under `checkpoint_2620`
- keep the RFC parser path unchanged and use a dedicated Check Point
fallback parser
- add regression tests for `parse_syslog()` and `read_syslog`

## Testing
- `uvx tenzir-test --root test-legacy --match checkpoint_dialect`
- `uvx tenzir-test --root test-legacy --match syslog`

## Changelog
- Added unreleased `feature` entry: "Check Point syslog structured-data
dialect parsing"

## Documentation PR
- https://github.com/tenzir/docs/pull/230

commit.message:
Port enumerate operator to new executor (#5859)

## Summary

- port `enumerate` to the new executor with `OperatorPlugin` +
`Describer`
- keep the legacy `operator_plugin2` implementation so non-neo execution
still works
- share grouping logic and preserve existing signed/unsigned integer
grouping behavior
- snapshot mutable executor state (`next_id`, grouped counters)

## Verification

- `cmake --build build/xcode/release --target tenzir --parallel 8`
- `uvx tenzir-test --root test-legacy --match operators/enumerate`
- manual smoke check of `test-legacy/tests/operators/enumerate.tql` with
and without `--neo`

## Changelog

- no changelog entry (internal port; no user-facing behavior change)

<sub>resolves TNZ-168</sub>

commit.message:
Clean up formatter API and config handling

Align formatter event handling with official type guards and remove
cast-based tool_result path extraction.

Use pi's getAgentDir() for formatter config lookup so
PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR is honored.

Improve path/file-kind handling by normalizing Unicode spaces and
expanding C/C++ extension detection.

Improve clang-format changed-line detection by considering both
staged and unstaged git diffs.

Simplify runner internals by removing unused SourceTool plumbing,
using ExecResult directly, and keeping Prettier as a reliable
fallback formatter for JSON/JS/TS.

Update README and changelog entries to reflect the new behavior.

commit.message:
Add configurable mcporter call output mode (#1)

* Add configurable mcporter call output mode

* Add changelog entry for mcporter output mode

* Fix mcporter output mode regressions

Respect pi's configured agent dir when reading and writing
mcporter.json so /mcporter persists across supported setups.

Keep expanded call output visible for shared and exported
sessions even when the interactive renderer is set to off.

Emit non-UI command feedback on stderr so /mcporter status
and mode changes stay visible in print and JSON runs.

* Use pi-native tool expansion

Remove the mcporter-specific output mode and follow pi's
existing expandTools behavior for collapsed and expanded
tool output.

Keep collapsed summaries for search, describe, and call,
and show the full rendered output when the user expands
tool results.

This simplifies the extension surface and avoids adding a
second output-mode configuration on top of pi.

* Add changelog entry for tool expansion

Record that pi-mcporter now follows pi's native tool
expansion behavior instead of maintaining a separate
output-mode setting.

truncated tool call previews so long-running jobs and clipped
inputs are easier to understand.

speculative web calls.

changelog.

commit.message:
Add web_contents support to Gemini provider

Use Gemini's built-in URL Context tool to extract the main
textual content from public URLs. The model reads each URL
server-side and returns cleaned page content with structure
preserved.

Retrieval metadata is included in the output so callers can
see which URLs succeeded and which hit paywall, error, or
safety blocks. A new contentsModel setting controls the model
used for extraction, defaulting to gemini-2.5-flash.

commit.message:
Fix changelog feed generation in CI

The changelog sync script uses uvx to run tenzir-ship when it
generates feeds and timeline data. Our docs workflow did not install
uv, so CI produced zero feeds and later failed link validation on
/changelog/feed.xml.

Install uv before generating the changelog so the build creates the
feed files consistently in CI.

commit.message:
Add init command for changelog setup (#15)

* Add init command for changelog setup

Add a dedicated CLI command to scaffold changelog projects.
It supports interactive and --yes flows, standalone and
package mode, and refuses to overwrite existing projects.

Add CLI tests for standalone initialization, package mode,
validation, and the existing-project error path.

* Fix init root resolution

Treat an empty changelog/ directory as the init target instead
of nesting another changelog/ below it.

Also derive standalone init defaults from the parent workspace
when the target root is a changelog/ directory, and cover both
cases with CLI regression tests.

* Sync tenzir-ship skill with init workflow

Add an explicit init-project workflow to the repo-local skill,
link add-entry guidance to the new setup path, and document in
AGENTS.md that skill references must stay aligned with key
user-facing workflows.

* Harden init confirmation flow

Initialize the confirmation flag defensively so the helper
remains safe even if abort_on_user_interrupt ever stops
raising. This also keeps static analysis happy.

* Allow init with a new --root path

Relax the global --root path constraint so explicit targets do
not need to exist before invocation, and preserve explicit
non-existent roots during context creation.

This keeps the init workflow aligned with the reference docs
and adds regression coverage for initializing into a new nested
changelog directory.

* Preserve --root validation outside init

Keep non-existent --root paths valid for , but restore the
old fail-fast behavior for other CLI commands before context
creation.

Add regression coverage for rejecting missing roots on
and  while still allowing  to create a new target
path.

* Cover explicit package roots in init tests

Add regression coverage showing that
still auto-detects package mode. This documents the intended behavior
and guards against future regressions in root resolution.

commit.message:
Parse structured data in legacy syslog

Extract RFC 5424-style structured data when RFC 3164 messages
prefix their content with bracketed elements. This fixes
vmware-style syslog records where Originator metadata stayed
inside content instead of becoming structured fields.

Keep ordinary RFC 3164 events on `syslog.rfc3164` and only emit
`structured_data` for records that actually have it.

commit.message:
Extract pull request resolver

Move GitHub remote parsing and pull request selection into a
dedicated module so git state collection stays focused on shelling
out and assembling footer data.

This keeps the PR lookup architecture easier to reason about,
centralizes the matching rules, and adds direct tests for repository
context derivation and strict head-owner filtering.

commit.message:
Harden pull request lookup

Avoid showing unrelated pull requests when forked branches share
the same name. The footer now accepts branch-search results only
when the head owner matches a known GitHub remote owner for the
current checkout, and otherwise falls back to the checkout-aware
GitHub CLI lookup.

Disable PR discovery entirely for repositories without GitHub
remotes so the footer stops issuing guaranteed-failing background
lookups. Add regression tests for both behaviors and expose a test
script for the package.

commit.message:
Fix fancy footer PR refresh

Resolve pull requests against likely base repositories instead of
assuming the tracked remote owns the PR. This lets fork-based
branches still surface the matching PR while keeping the current
branch fallback for direct lookups.

Split PR fetching out of the local git refresh path so branch,
commit, and diff metadata render immediately. The PR widget now
refreshes independently with its own cache and stale-result guards.

commit.message:
Restrict SSH remote fetch to guarded branches only

The SSH remote (origin-ssh) was configured with a wildcard fetch
refspec, so every topic branch appeared on both origin and origin-ssh.
This caused 'matched multiple remote tracking branches' errors when
running git checkout without an explicit --track flag.

setup-guarded-pushes now replaces the SSH remote's fetch refspec with
one entry per guarded branch. restrict_ssh_remote_fetch is called on
every run, so re-runs and branch-list changes stay in sync.

record_original_state_if_unmanaged saves the original SSH fetch
refspec under hooks.protectedPushOriginalSshFetch. unguard-pushes
restores it when tearing down a pre-existing SSH remote and cleans
up the key on completion.

commit.message:
Track guarded branches via SSH remote

Guarded branches set pushRemote to the SSH remote but left
branch.<name>.remote pointing at the HTTPS remote. After pushing,
git status showed the branch as ahead because the tracking ref
(origin/main) was never updated—only origin-ssh/main was.

Now setup-guarded-pushes also switches branch.<name>.remote to
the SSH remote so tracking follows the push path. unguard-pushes
restores it to the primary remote on teardown.

commit.message:
Serialize TCP sink connection handling

Refactor serve_tcp and to_tcp to route payload handling through a single manager path instead of sharing transports across callbacks. This removes the ad-hoc Box/Arc ownership split in to_tcp and keeps serve_tcp on the same serialized model.

The result is simpler connection state, no lock choreography around async writes, and easier follow-up work on TCP runtime metrics.

commit.message:
Serialize serve_tcp client handling

Route accepted sockets and outgoing payloads through the same
manager queue so one task owns the live client set. This drops
shared client ownership and locking while keeping exact
max_connections accounting with the reservation counter.

The new structure is easier to reason about and avoids copying
client handles on every broadcast.

plumbing across accept_tcp, from_tcp, and to_tcp.

commit.message:
Fix TCP reader lifetime bugs

Keep the accept_tcp and from_tcp reader state alive across spawned
coroutines and close transports on the owning event-base thread.

This also switches the read path away from IOBuf queue conversion and
reads directly into byte buffers, which avoids the crashes seen in the
plain TCP operator tests.

commit.message:
Simplify TCP operator lifecycles

Remove dead stop() overrides from the TCP operators and
collapse duplicated connection shutdown paths where the same
cleanup already runs through finalize() or a shared close event.

For accept_tcp and from_tcp, read loops now emit a single terminal
connection message that optionally carries an error. This removes
duplicate close notifications and keeps connection teardown in one
place.

TCP integration tests for accept_tcp, from_tcp, serve_tcp, and to_tcp
pass after the simplification.

commit.message:
Fix TCP operator shutdown

Make the TCP accept loops stop reliably during shutdown.

The previous code called server_->close() while an accept coroutine
could still be suspended. Folly's ServerSocket::accept() does not wake
up just because accepting stops, so shutdown could depend on later
scope cancellation. Closing the socket also needs to happen on the
socket event-base thread.

This change adds an explicit cancellation source for the accept loops,
runs server shutdown on the event-base thread, and treats
OperationCancelled as a normal exit path. It applies the same fix to
accept_tcp and serve_tcp.

TCP integration tests for accept_tcp, from_tcp, serve_tcp, and to_tcp
pass with this change.

commit.message:
Fix accept_tcp draining shutdown

Stop promoting already-queued accepted sockets once shutdown enters
draining mode. During teardown, accept_tcp now closes those
connections immediately and releases their reserved slots instead
of spawning fresh subpipelines that prolong termination.

commit.message:
Fix TCP operator shutdown behavior

Make accept_tcp drain structurally during finalize instead of
relying on ad-hoc completion checks. The operator now stops
accepting new connections, tracks a draining lifecycle, and
finishes once all accepted work has been released.

Also let cancellation propagate in from_tcp and serve_tcp,
and guard to_tcp's shared transport state so reconnects and
subpipeline writes cannot race.

commit.message:
Fix SBOM upload authentication

Use the caller workflow's built-in GitHub token for the SBOM upload
step instead of relying on a token output from the reusable release
workflow. This keeps the SBOM job independent from reusable workflow
internals and ensures release uploads succeed reliably.

commit.message:
Simplify guarded push config

Replace the managed two-remote setup with a single-origin workflow.
Guarded branches now switch to SSH via includeIf.onbranch while
normal fetches and topic pushes stay on HTTPS.

Keep the pre-push hook as a backstop, add a status helper, and
cover the new behavior with round-trip tests.

commit.message:
Add Homebrew tap release automation (#5876)

## Summary
- normalize the macOS pkg identifier used by the signed installer
package
- add release automation that updates `tenzir/homebrew-tenzir` from the
signed macOS package artifact
- add a small helper to render the managed Homebrew cask contents

## Testing
- python3 -m py_compile .github/workflows/nix-build.py
scripts/update-homebrew-cask.py
- ruby -e 'require "yaml";
YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/tenzir.yaml"); puts "ok"'
- brew audit --cask --online --strict tenzir/homebrew-tenzir/tenzir
- smoke test the identifier rewrite against the real `v5.28.0` macOS
package

<sub>resolves TNZ-374</sub>

author Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1773778821 +0100
committer Matthias Vallentin <matthias@vallentin.net> 1773778821 +0100

Release 0.1.0

commit.message:
Add agent workflow runtime (#1)

* Add agent composition runtime

Add a serializable composition runtime on top of the existing
subprocess-backed agent execution path.

This keeps the standalone agent tool intact while introducing a new
compose tool, persistent composition lifecycle state, and a basic
composition UI surface.

The runtime supports spawn, sequence, fork, join, and loop flows,
plus budget checks and session reconstruction. It also adds tests for
composition behavior and updates existing tests for the refactor.

* Refine workflow run naming

Keep workflow as the public term for agent workflow definitions,
but use run as the user-facing term for live executions.

This renames the inspection command from /workflows to /runs,
updates the runtime UI copy, refreshes delegated context wording,
and aligns the README, changelog, and tests with the new naming.

* Split agent and run inspection commands

Make /agents and /runs list-only commands and add /agent and\n/run for inspecting a single item. This matches the command\nlayout users expect and gives clearer usage hints when list\ncommands receive an argument.\n\nUpdate the command tests and README so the documented and\nverified behavior stays in sync.

* Rename workflow compositions to runs

Replace the internal composition runtime terminology with run-based\nnames throughout the workflow engine, state, persistence layer,\nand public TypeScript types. This aligns the implementation with\nthe /runs and /run user-facing model.\n\nThis is a hard cut: remove composition aliases, composition event\nnames, and composition-specific environment variables. Update the\nREADME, changelog entry, and tests to match the new terminology.

* Serialize budget mutations through actor

Replace the shared mutable BudgetSnapshot with a BudgetActor that
serializes every read and mutation through a FIFO promise-chain
mailbox. Concurrent fork branches now safely share the same actor
without risking interleaved check-then-act operations.

The assertDepth + consumeChild pair becomes a single atomic
acquireSpawn message so no budget slot can be consumed between
the depth check and the child counter increment.

* Simplify README terminology

Remove the separate Flow Spec and Budgets concept sections in favor
of inline descriptions under Workflow and Runs. Replace references
to 'flow spec' with 'node tree' or 'workflow' throughout.

* Scope node counter per execute() call

Move the nodeCounter from the RunExecutor instance into a closure
inside execute() so concurrent or sequential workflow runs never
share ID state. The counter and its createNodeId closure are
threaded through EvaluationState.

Also remove the history-scanning fallback from resolveContinueValue
and document the resolution priority. The body result and its
summary are sufficient; probing the full reversed history could
silently match stale values from prior iterations.

* Remove unused join methods from AgentManager

The joinAll, joinAny, and joinQuorum methods were never called.
The executor implements its own fork/join logic directly.

* Improve /agents and /runs argument handling

When the user passes an argument to /agents or /runs, show a short
redirect hint instead of dumping the full list alongside an error.

* Document optional id fields for sequence and join

Add id field notes to the sequence and join sections so the
reference is consistent with spawn, fork, and loop.

* Add test for join agent reducer

Exercise the reducer: { kind: 'agent' } path in evaluateJoin,
which delegates fork summarization to a separate agent. Assert
that the reducer receives the branch results and its output
becomes the join result.

* Fix workflow run concurrency gaps

Make workflow cancellation and fork fail-fast behavior reliable.
Agent and workflow runs now share the same run runtime, direct
agent delegations appear in /runs, and run updates expose stable
snapshots instead of live mutable state.

Add regression coverage for fail-fast fork cancellation, the
post-abort spawn race, immutable snapshots, and direct agent run
persistence.

* Improve agent extension integration

Align the agent extension more closely with Pi core APIs.

Remove the defensive session_start guard, add typed event channel
constants, and provide custom tool call and result rendering for the
agent and workflow tools.

Also make unknown agents fail through the normal tool error path
instead of returning an ad-hoc isError field, and cover that behavior
in tests.

* Inherit parent model in delegated runs

Delegated agent and workflow spawns now fall back to the active
session model and thinking level when the agent definition omits
those fields.

This keeps the runtime behavior aligned with the tool output and
agent details, and avoids silently using the child process default
configuration instead of the caller's active session settings.

* Rebuild run state on session changes

The run widget and /runs commands now rebuild from the active
branch on session start, session switches, forks, and tree
navigation.

This keeps the extension aligned with pi's branching/session
abstractions instead of holding on to stale run state from a
previous branch or session.

* Honor delegated cwd for agent lookup

The agent tool now resolves project-local agents against the same
cwd that the delegated subprocess uses.

This prevents cross-project delegations from being validated in one
workspace and executed in another, which could reject valid agents
or pick the wrong project-local definition.

* Validate workflow ids and joins

Workflow validation now enforces unique node ids across the flow
and checks that every join references a fork node.

This prevents silent overwrites in execution memory and catches
invalid join wiring before any delegated agents start running.

* Bound workflow handoff context

Downstream workflow spawns now receive compact context previews
instead of the full nested outputs from every prior step.

This removes duplicated text, keeps named-node context bounded,
and truncates oversized payloads before they are appended to the
next delegated task prompt.

* Expose a typed workflow schema

The workflow tool now publishes a recursive TypeBox schema for
flow definitions instead of an untyped blob.

This gives tool callers structured guidance for spawn, sequence,
fork, join, and loop nodes before runtime validation runs.

* Import the shared thinking level type

Agent discovery now reuses the shared ThinkingLevel type instead
of redefining the same string union locally.

This keeps the frontmatter model aligned with pi's core agent
types and removes one source of drift in the extension.

* Deduplicate diagnostic formatting

Shared diagnostic text formatting now lives in one helper module
instead of being redefined in both the extension entrypoint and
the workflow executor.

This keeps agent discovery diagnostics consistent everywhere they
are rendered or passed into delegated runs.

* Extract tool schemas from the extension entry

The entrypoint now imports its tool schemas and extension-specific
error types from a dedicated module instead of defining them inline.

This trims down index.ts and separates tool metadata from runtime
wiring and execution logic.

* Extract presentation helpers from index

Rendering, formatting, and run-widget presentation logic now live
in a dedicated module instead of being mixed into the extension
entrypoint.

This leaves index.ts focused on wiring tools, commands, and runtime
behavior while presentation concerns stay isolated.

* Drop unused context from spawn engines

SpawnEngine now only receives the data it actually uses through
SpawnRequest instead of carrying an unused ExtensionContext
parameter through the subprocess path.

This tightens the engine abstraction and makes it clearer which
inputs delegated spawn implementations depend on.

* Clean up executor readability and drop unused dep

- Simplify resolveContinueValue to a two-step lookup (output.<path>
  then raw <path>), remove unused memory parameter and stale comment.
- Rename primaryFailure() to getPrimaryFailure() so the method no
  longer shadows the state field name.
- Remove no-op catch block in evaluateSpawn that only re-threw.
- Drop unused DelegatedAgentRunError import left behind by the
  catch removal.
- Remove phantom @mariozechner/pi-agent-core dev dependency that
  was never imported.

* Fix workflow join semantics

Short-circuit fork execution when joins use any or quorum so\nworkflows can proceed as soon as the success condition is met.\n\nReject joins that target future or out-of-scope forks and disallow\nmultiple joins for the same fork to keep execution behavior\nunambiguous.

* Fix TUI line wrapping in agent renderer

Wrap custom agent and workflow render output to the terminal
width so long task and result text no longer crashes the TUI.

Add regression tests that assert rendered lines stay within
the requested width for narrow terminals.

* Fix duplicate run spinners in TUI

Keep the global Working spinner as the only animated indicator.
Render the runs widget with static status markers and collapse the
redundant child row for single spawn runs that repeat the run label.

Also add a regression test for the simplified runs widget output.

* Add deterministic Mermaid diagram export

Add toMermaid(flow, options?) that converts a FlowSpec into a Mermaid
flowchart string. The same input always produces the same output:
node IDs are counter-based (n0, n1, …), fork branches are visited
in sorted key order, and class definitions follow a fixed order.

Node shapes encode the flow kind: spawn as stadium, fork and join as
diamonds, and loop as hexagon. Color-coded classDef styles distinguish
the node types visually.

* Add flow tree visualization and /flow command

Add formatFlowTree() that renders a FlowSpec as an ASCII tree with
kind icons: ● spawn, ◇ fork, ◆ join, ◎ loop. Sequences are
transparent — their children inline at the parent indentation level.
When runtime state is available, status icons replace kind icons:
✔ completed, ✘ failed, ⠹ running, ◌ waiting, ■ aborted, ○ queued.

Register /flow [id] command for inspecting a run's structure as an
ASCII tree, with /flow [id] mermaid for Mermaid export. Enhance
renderWorkflowCall to show the flow tree inline when the workflow
tool is invoked.

Replace the static string-array widget with a timer-driven
RunWidgetManager that cycles braille spinner frames at 80 ms,
following the pi-subagents pattern. Single-spawn runs show only
the header line to avoid redundancy. Multi-node workflows show the
full flow tree with animated status overlay.

* Skip duplicate root label in widget flow tree

When the flow tree's root node label matches the run header, omit
it to avoid showing the same name twice.

* Resolve status icons for nodes without spec IDs

Extend the node index to also look up RunNodes by label and branch
key. This lets flowIcon() resolve status for spawn nodes that the
LLM created without an explicit id field, so fork branches show
animated spinners instead of static kind icons.

* Replace footer status with run counts event

Remove the setStatus call from the widget and emit a
RUN_COUNTS_CHANGED event with {runs, running, waiting, queued}
on every state change. Other extensions can listen to this event
and display the counters wherever they see fit.

* Improve workflow authoring and validation

Add compact workflow syntax, normalize agent references, and
inject a discovered workflow agent catalog into orchestration
prompts. The runtime now preflights workflow agent references
before spawning children, which cuts retry churn and makes
workflow authoring more reliable.

* Polish workflow rendering

Render compact summaries for structured workflow outputs,
hide transient result text while runs are still active, and
use canonical spec paths to overlay branch and loop status
reliably in the flow tree.

* Wrap agent and workflow results in XML envelopes

Return tool results in structured XML so the orchestrating LLM
can parse multi-branch outputs more reliably. Fork results use
<branch> and <error> elements; simple outputs get a plain wrapper.

TUI renderers strip the envelope before display so users see
clean text.

* Fix XML result envelopes

Escape agent and workflow result payloads before embedding them in XML envelopes so delegated output cannot break the wrapper structure.

Serialize workflow outputs losslessly inside explicit <output> elements instead of reinterpreting arbitrary JSON objects based on their shape. This preserves user payloads such as objects with branches or errors fields.

Add regression tests for XML-looking payloads and lossless workflow JSON round-tripping.

* Add detachable workflow runs

Let Escape detach already-started agent and workflow runs so they\ncontinue in the background instead of being terminated with the\nforeground operation.\n\nAdd explicit lifecycle control through /runs and /run stop, keep\ndetached runs persisted against their origin session, and surface the\ndetached state in run details.\n\nHide detached runs from the live widget so the TUI only shows\nforeground-attached work while background runs remain available through\nslash commands.

* Unify flow inspection and watch mode

Make flow the public execution surface instead of the old\nrun split. Add /flows for browsing, turn /flow into the\nprimary inspect command, and support omitted IDs through an\ninteractive picker in TUI mode.\n\nAdd /flow watch for re-entering live execution with an\nanimated tree, explicit stop handling, and detach semantics\nthat match the foreground/background UX. Update user-facing\ncopy, docs, and tests to match the new flow model.

* Add detached workflow notifications

* Use status icons in workflow notifications

* Refine flow inspection presentation

* Show static flow structure in inspect view

The inspect output now shows the flow structure using static kind
icons instead of overlaying runtime status. This makes the structure
section a stable reference of what the flow looks like, independent
of how far execution has progressed.

Runtime status is still visible in the Nodes summary line and the
Latest node field.

* Add expand/collapse hints to run notifications

Collapsed notifications now truncate long summaries and errors to
four lines and show a key hint for expanding. Expanded notifications
show a hint for collapsing. Notifications without meaningful extra
detail skip the hint entirely.

* Skip last-step summary in final sequence notification

For multi-step sequences the final run notification no longer
repeats the last step's output as its summary. Each step already
posts its own notification, so duplicating the last one in the
final message added noise without new information.

* Add Unicode flow diagram via beautiful-mermaid

Adds renderFlowAscii() which converts a FlowSpec to Mermaid text
and renders it as Unicode box-drawing art using beautiful-mermaid.
Supports a plain ASCII fallback and configurable ANSI color modes.

The diagram is available through /flow diagram <id>, the d shortcut
in the flow picker, and the Diagram option in the /flows menu.

* Connect root sequence siblings visually

Root-level sequences with multiple steps were rendered without tree
connectors, making joins appear disconnected from their forks. Now
multi-step root sequences use the same connector lines as nested
sequences, tying siblings together with │ continuation lines.

* Remove redundant arrows from flow tree

Drop → in fork branches and ← in join labels. The tree structure
already conveys which agent handles a branch and which fork a join
collects from. Branch lines now use a colon separator instead.

* Let icons carry node types in flow trees

Drop the redundant 'join:' prefix from join labels since ◆ already
signals the type. Swap branch lines to show the agent first and
branch key second, matching the consistent pattern where the icon
conveys the node type and the text identifies the instance.

* Color-code flow tree icons and dim connectors

Status icons get semantic colors (success, error, accent, warning,
dim) and kind icons get accent coloring to stand out from the tree
chrome. Tree-drawing characters (├─ └─ │) are dimmed to recede
visually. Coloring is applied only when a theme is available;
plain-text callers like formatFlowInspectText are unaffected.

* Add node result summaries to flow inspect

* Show live flow results while watching

Surface node outputs directly in /flow watch so users can follow
agent results without detaching first.

Also extract reusable node result formatting for inspect and watch
views, add presentation coverage, and keep the full result history
visible while a flow is still running.

* Restore expand hints for previewed output

Show expand and collapse hints again for truncated preview sections
in agent and workflow renderers.

Also render the 'more lines' suffix in dim styling so previewed
output matches the usual visual treatment and is less confusing.

* Fix changelog example and add claude as co-author

Sync the review-loop example with the README: use typed field
annotations in the reviewer task prompt and add explicit output:text
to the engineer spawn. Also add claude to the authors list.

* Refine workflow run naming

Keep workflow as the public term for agent workflow definitions,
but use run as the user-facing term for live executions.

This renames the inspection command from /workflows to /runs,
updates the runtime UI copy, refreshes delegated context wording,
and aligns the README, changelog, and tests with the new naming.

* Split agent and run inspection commands

Make /agents and /runs list-only commands and add /agent and\n/run for inspecting a single item. This matches the command\nlayout users expect and gives clearer usage hints when list\ncommands receive an argument.\n\nUpdate the command tests and README so the documented and\nverified behavior stays in sync.

* Rename workflow compositions to runs

Replace the internal composition runtime terminology with run-based\nnames throughout the workflow engine, state, persistence layer,\nand public TypeScript types. This aligns the implementation with\nthe /runs and /run user-facing model.\n\nThis is a hard cut: remove composition aliases, composition event\nnames, and composition-specific environment variables. Update the\nREADME, changelog entry, and tests to match the new terminology.

* Serialize budget mutations through actor

Replace the shared mutable BudgetSnapshot with a BudgetActor that
serializes every read and mutation through a FIFO promise-chain
mailbox. Concurrent fork branches now safely share the same actor
without risking interleaved check-then-act operations.

The assertDepth + consumeChild pair becomes a single atomic
acquireSpawn message so no budget slot can be consumed between
the depth check and the child counter increment.

* Simplify README terminology

Remove the separate Flow Spec and Budgets concept sections in favor
of inline descriptions under Workflow and Runs. Replace references
to 'flow spec' with 'node tree' or 'workflow' throughout.

* Scope node counter per execute() call

Move the nodeCounter from the RunExecutor instance into a closure
inside execute() so concurrent or sequential workflow runs never
share ID state. The counter and its createNodeId closure are
threaded through EvaluationState.

Also remove the history-scanning fallback from resolveContinueValue
and document the resolution priority. The body result and its
summary are sufficient; probing the full reversed history could
silently match stale values from prior iterations.

* Remove unused join methods from AgentManager

The joinAll, joinAny, and joinQuorum methods were never called.
The executor implements its own fork/join logic directly.

* Improve /agents and /runs argument handling

When the user passes an argument to /agents or /runs, show a short
redirect hint instead of dumping the full list alongside an error.

* Document optional id fields for sequence and join

Add id field notes to the sequence and join sections so the
reference is consistent with spawn, fork, and loop.

* Add test for join agent reducer

Exercise the reducer: { kind: 'agent' } path in evaluateJoin,
which delegates fork summarization to a separate agent. Assert
that the reducer receives the branch results and its output
becomes the join result.

* Fix workflow run concurrency gaps

Make workflow cancellation and fork fail-fast behavior reliable.
Agent and workflow runs now share the same run runtime, direct
agent delegations appear in /runs, and run updates expose stable
snapshots instead of live mutable state.

Add regression coverage for fail-fast fork cancellation, the
post-abort spawn race, immutable snapshots, and direct agent run
persistence.

* Improve agent extension integration

Align the agent extension more closely with Pi core APIs.

Remove the defensive session_start guard, add typed event channel
constants, and provide custom tool call and result rendering for the
agent and workflow tools.

Also make unknown agents fail through the normal tool error path
instead of returning an ad-hoc isError field, and cover that behavior
in tests.

* Inherit parent model in delegated runs

Delegated agent and workflow spawns now fall back to the active
session model and thinking level when the agent definition omits
those fields.

This keeps the runtime behavior aligned with the tool output and
agent details, and avoids silently using the child process default
configuration instead of the caller's active session settings.

* Rebuild run state on session changes

The run widget and /runs commands now rebuild from the active
branch on session start, session switches, forks, and tree
navigation.

This keeps the extension aligned with pi's branching/session
abstractions instead of holding on to stale run state from a
previous branch or session.

* Honor delegated cwd for agent lookup

The agent tool now resolves project-local agents against the same
cwd that the delegated subprocess uses.

This prevents cross-project delegations from being validated in one
workspace and executed in another, which could reject valid agents
or pick the wrong project-local definition.

* Validate workflow ids and joins

Workflow validation now enforces unique node ids across the flow
and checks that every join references a fork node.

This prevents silent overwrites in execution memory and catches
invalid join wiring before any delegated agents start running.

* Bound workflow handoff context

Downstream workflow spawns now receive compact context previews
instead of the full nested outputs from every prior step.

This removes duplicated text, keeps named-node context bounded,
and truncates oversized payloads before they are appended to the
next delegated task prompt.

* Expose a typed workflow schema

The workflow tool now publishes a recursive TypeBox schema for
flow definitions instead of an untyped blob.

This gives tool callers structured guidance for spawn, sequence,
fork, join, and loop nodes before runtime validation runs.

* Import the shared thinking level type

Agent discovery now reuses the shared ThinkingLevel type instead
of redefining the same string union locally.

This keeps the frontmatter model aligned with pi's core agent
types and removes one source of drift in the extension.

* Deduplicate diagnostic formatting

Shared diagnostic text formatting now lives in one helper module
instead of being redefined in both the extension entrypoint and
the workflow executor.

This keeps agent discovery diagnostics consistent everywhere they
are rendered or passed into delegated runs.

* Extract tool schemas from the extension entry

The entrypoint now imports its tool schemas and extension-specific
error types from a dedicated module instead of defining them inline.

This trims down index.ts and separates tool metadata from runtime
wiring and execution logic.

* Extract presentation helpers from index

Rendering, formatting, and run-widget presentation logic now live
in a dedicated module instead of being mixed into the extension
entrypoint.

This leaves index.ts focused on wiring tools, commands, and runtime
behavior while presentation concerns stay isolated.

* Drop unused context from spawn engines

SpawnEngine now only receives the data it actually uses through
SpawnRequest instead of carrying an unused ExtensionContext
parameter through the subprocess path.

This tightens the engine abstraction and makes it clearer which
inputs delegated spawn implementations depend on.

* Clean up executor readability and drop unused dep

- Simplify resolveContinueValue to a two-step lookup (output.<path>
  then raw <path>), remove unused memory parameter and stale comment.
- Rename primaryFailure() to getPrimaryFailure() so the method no
  longer shadows the state field name.
- Remove no-op catch block in evaluateSpawn that only re-threw.
- Drop unused DelegatedAgentRunError import left behind by the
  catch removal.
- Remove phantom @mariozechner/pi-agent-core dev dependency that
  was never imported.

* Fix workflow join semantics

Short-circuit fork execution when joins use any or quorum so\nworkflows can proceed as soon as the success condition is met.\n\nReject joins that target future or out-of-scope forks and disallow\nmultiple joins for the same fork to keep execution behavior\nunambiguous.

* Fix TUI line wrapping in agent renderer

Wrap custom agent and workflow render output to the terminal
width so long task and result text no longer crashes the TUI.

Add regression tests that assert rendered lines stay within
the requested width for narrow terminals.

* Fix duplicate run spinners in TUI

Keep the global Working spinner as the only animated indicator.
Render the runs widget with static status markers and collapse the
redundant child row for single spawn runs that repeat the run label.

Also add a regression test for the simplified runs widget output.

* Add deterministic Mermaid diagram export

Add toMermaid(flow, options?) that converts a FlowSpec into a Mermaid
flowchart string. The same input always produces the same output:
node IDs are counter-based (n0, n1, …), fork branches are visited
in sorted key order, and class definitions follow a fixed order.

Node shapes encode the flow kind: spawn as stadium, fork and join as
diamonds, and loop as hexagon. Color-coded classDef styles distinguish
the node types visually.

* Add flow tree visualization and /flow command

Add formatFlowTree() that renders a FlowSpec as an ASCII tree with
kind icons: ● spawn, ◇ fork, ◆ join, ◎ loop. Sequences are
transparent — their children inline at the parent indentation level.
When runtime state is available, status icons replace kind icons:
✔ completed, ✘ failed, ⠹ running, ◌ waiting, ■ aborted, ○ queued.

Register /flow [id] command for inspecting a run's structure as an
ASCII tree, with /flow [id] mermaid for Mermaid export. Enhance
renderWorkflowCall to show the flow tree inline when the workflow
tool is invoked.

Replace the static string-array widget with a timer-driven
RunWidgetManager that cycles braille spinner frames at 80 ms,
following the pi-subagents pattern. Single-spawn runs show only
the header line to avoid redundancy. Multi-node workflows show the
full flow tree with animated status overlay.

* Skip duplicate root label in widget flow tree

When the flow tree's root node label matches the run header, omit
it to avoid showing the same name twice.

* Resolve status icons for nodes without spec IDs

Extend the node index to also look up RunNodes by label and branch
key. This lets flowIcon() resolve status for spawn nodes that the
LLM created without an explicit id field, so fork branches show
animated spinners instead of static kind icons.

* Replace footer status with run counts event

Remove the setStatus call from the widget and emit a
RUN_COUNTS_CHANGED event with {runs, running, waiting, queued}
on every state change. Other extensions can listen to this event
and display the counters wherever they see fit.

* Improve workflow authoring and validation

Add compact workflow syntax, normalize agent references, and
inject a discovered workflow agent catalog into orchestration
prompts. The runtime now preflights workflow agent references
before spawning children, which cuts retry churn and makes
workflow authoring more reliable.

* Polish workflow rendering

Render compact summaries for structured workflow outputs,
hide transient result text while runs are still active, and
use canonical spec paths to overlay branch and loop status
reliably in the flow tree.

* Wrap agent and workflow results in XML envelopes

Return tool results in structured XML so the orchestrating LLM
can parse multi-branch outputs more reliably. Fork results use
<branch> and <error> elements; simple outputs get a plain wrapper.

TUI renderers strip the envelope before display so users see
clean text.

* Fix XML result envelopes

Escape agent and workflow result payloads before embedding them in XML envelopes so delegated output cannot break the wrapper structure.

Serialize workflow outputs losslessly inside explicit <output> elements instead of reinterpreting arbitrary JSON objects based on their shape. This preserves user payloads such as objects with branches or errors fields.

Add regression tests for XML-looking payloads and lossless workflow JSON round-tripping.

* Add detachable workflow runs

Let Escape detach already-started agent and workflow runs so they\ncontinue in the background instead of being terminated with the\nforeground operation.\n\nAdd explicit lifecycle control through /runs and /run stop, keep\ndetached runs persisted against their origin session, and surface the\ndetached state in run details.\n\nHide detached runs from the live widget so the TUI only shows\nforeground-attached work while background runs remain available through\nslash commands.

* Unify flow inspection and watch mode

Make flow the public execution surface instead of the old\nrun split. Add /flows for browsing, turn /flow into the\nprimary inspect command, and support omitted IDs through an\ninteractive picker in TUI mode.\n\nAdd /flow watch for re-entering live execution with an\nanimated tree, explicit stop handling, and detach semantics\nthat match the foreground/background UX. Update user-facing\ncopy, docs, and tests to match the new flow model.

* Add detached workflow notifications

* Use status icons in workflow notifications

* Refine flow inspection presentation

* Show static flow structure in inspect view

The inspect output now shows the flow structure using static kind
icons instead of overlaying runtime status. This makes the structure
section a stable reference of what the flow looks like, independent
of how far execution has progressed.

Runtime status is still visible in the Nodes summary line and the
Latest node field.

* Add expand/collapse hints to run notifications

Collapsed notifications now truncate long summaries and errors to
four lines and show a key hint for expanding. Expanded notifications
show a hint for collapsing. Notifications without meaningful extra
detail skip the hint entirely.

* Skip last-step summary in final sequence notification

For multi-step sequences the final run notification no longer
repeats the last step's output as its summary. Each step already
posts its own notification, so duplicating the last one in the
final message added noise without new information.

* Add Unicode flow diagram via beautiful-mermaid

Adds renderFlowAscii() which converts a FlowSpec to Mermaid text
and renders it as Unicode box-drawing art using beautiful-mermaid.
Supports a plain ASCII fallback and configurable ANSI color modes.

The diagram is available through /flow diagram <id>, the d shortcut
in the flow picker, and the Diagram option in the /flows menu.

* Connect root sequence siblings visually

Root-level sequences with multiple steps were rendered without tree
connectors, making joins appear disconnected from their forks. Now
multi-step root sequences use the same connector lines as nested
sequences, tying siblings together with │ continuation lines.

* Remove redundant arrows from flow tree

Drop → in fork branches and ← in join labels. The tree structure
already conveys which agent handles a branch and which fork a join
collects from. Branch lines now use a colon separator instead.

* Let icons carry node types in flow trees

Drop the redundant 'join:' prefix from join labels since ◆ already
signals the type. Swap branch lines to show the agent first and
branch key second, matching the consistent pattern where the icon
conveys the node type and the text identifies the instance.

* Color-code flow tree icons and dim connectors

Status icons get semantic colors (success, error, accent, warning,
dim) and kind icons get accent coloring to stand out from the tree
chrome. Tree-drawing characters (├─ └─ │) are dimmed to recede
visually. Coloring is applied only when a theme is available;
plain-text callers like formatFlowInspectText are unaffected.

* Add node result summaries to flow inspect

* Show live flow results while watching

Surface node outputs directly in /flow watch so users can follow
agent results without detaching first.

Also extract reusable node result formatting for inspect and watch
views, add presentation coverage, and keep the full result history
visible while a flow is still running.

* Restore expand hints for previewed output

Show expand and collapse hints again for truncated preview sections
in agent and workflow renderers.

Also render the 'more lines' suffix in dim styling so previewed
output matches the usual visual treatment and is less confusing.

* Fix changelog example and add claude as co-author

Sync the review-loop example with the README: use typed field
annotations in the reviewer task prompt and add explicit output:text
to the engineer spawn. Also add claude to the authors list.

commit.message:
Refactor TCP operators to follow executor patterns (#5915)

Align the TCP operators with the executor coordination patterns so
listener state stays queue-driven, `serve_tcp` no longer serializes TLS
handshakes on the accept loop, and the remaining TCP operators use the
owning event base explicitly.

### Review

- `accept_tcp` and `serve_tcp`
- replace ad-hoc listener coordination with explicit slot ownership and
task handles
  - keep accept-side work out of operator member mutation paths
- `serve_tcp`
- move TLS preparation into per-connection helper tasks before posting
accepted clients back into the queue
- `accept_tcp` and `from_tcp`
- store immutable configuration in `args_` to match the executor
guidance
- `serve_tcp` and `to_tcp`
  - dispatch writes on each transport's owning `EventBase`

### Details

<details>
<summary>🧪 Testing</summary>

- `cmake --build build/xcode/release --target tenzir -j8`
- `uvx tenzir-test --root test --match 'operators/accept_tcp'`
- `uvx tenzir-test --root test --match 'operators/from_tcp'`
- `uvx tenzir-test --root test --match 'operators/serve_tcp'`
- `uvx tenzir-test --root test --match 'operators/to_tcp'`

</details>

<details>
<summary>📌 References</summary>

- No changelog entry: internal refactoring only, with no intended
user-facing behavior change.
- No docs PR needed.

</details>

commit.message:
Make syslog parsing lenient for long SD names (#5922)

Accept syslog messages whose structured-data parameter names exceed the
RFC 5424 32-character limit. This fixes TNZ-462 and keeps vendor-emitted
messages parseable in both `parse_syslog()` and `read_syslog`.

### Review

- The behavior change is intentionally narrow: only the parameter-name
length cap was removed.
- Existing character restrictions and all other syslog field limits stay
unchanged.
- Added regression coverage for both public parsing surfaces.

### Details

<details>
<summary>🧪 Testing</summary>

- `scripts/build.sh`
- `PATH="$PWD/build/xcode/release/bin:$PATH" uvx tenzir-test --root
test-legacy --match tests/functions/parse_syslog --debug`
- `PATH="$PWD/build/xcode/release/bin:$PATH" uvx tenzir-test --root
test-legacy --match tests/operators/read_syslog --debug`

</details>

<details>
<summary>📌 References</summary>

- TNZ-462

</details>

N/M so they do not look like completion counters.

commit.message:

commit.message:
Simplify contents provider responses

Return contents as direct per-URL answers instead of provider-specific
formatted text.

This removes noisy operation type names, drops redundant content
discriminators, and lets contents providers preserve raw provider data
with a simple 1:1 mapping to the requested URLs.

It also updates caching, rendering, custom provider docs, and tests to
match the simpler contents model.

commit.message:
Handle Gemini research terminal states

Treat Gemini interaction statuses like requires_action and
incomplete as terminal research failures instead of polling until
the outer timeout.

This surfaces the real provider state earlier and avoids misleading
30-minute timeout errors when Gemini has already stopped making
forward progress.

commit.message:
Simplify web contents caching

Replace the generic content store with a small in-memory cache
and keep only the behaviors that matter for users: background
prefetch, per-URL reuse, TTL expiry, and session reset safety.

This also drops prefetch job tracking and prefetch ids, which were
internal complexity without a surfaced user workflow.
